description: V2 simplified test cases — no rule_checks. Evaluated via Claude LLM-only judge with auto-generated rubric.

test_cases.name: List — bullet must use LevelFormat not unicode
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a bulleted list of 5 items: 'Design', 'Develop', 'Test', 'Deploy', 'Monitor'. Use proper Word list formatting.
test_cases.expected_behavior: File uses native Word numbering (w:numPr), NOT unicode bullet characters inserted as text

test_cases.name: Table — dual widths + DXA + ShadingType.CLEAR
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a 3-column table (Name, Department, Salary) with 4 data rows. Add a blue-grey shaded header row. Use US Letter page size.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Table has columnWidths in DXA, each cell has explicit DXA width, shading uses CLEAR type, no PERCENTAGE widths

test_cases.name: Heading styles — outlineLevel required for ToC
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with 3 sections using Heading 1 for section titles and Heading 2 for subsections. Add a Table of Contents on the first page. Sections: Introduction (2 subs), Methodology (3 subs), Results (2 subs).
test_cases.expected_behavior: ToC generated from headings; if custom styles used, outlineLevel is present in paragraphStyles. ToC uses HeadingLevel enum not custom style names.

test_cases.name: Page break — must be inside Paragraph
test_cases.prompt: Create a 3-page Word document where each page has a distinct section title and body text. Force page breaks between sections.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Page breaks are valid — each PageBreak is a child of a Paragraph element, not standalone. File passes validate.py.

test_cases.name: No \n in paragraphs — separate Paragraph objects
test_cases.prompt: Create a memo Word document with To, From, Date, Subject fields on separate lines, followed by 3 body paragraphs.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Each field and each paragraph is a separate Paragraph element. No \n characters inside TextRun content.

test_cases.name: Footer with tab stops — not table
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a footer containing: company name on the left, document title centered, page number on the right.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Footer uses tab stops (TabStopType.CENTER and TabStopType.RIGHT) not a Table. No table elements inside footer XML.

test_cases.name: ImageRun — type parameter required
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a placeholder company logo image (generate a simple 100x50px colored rectangle as PNG) and a title 'Annual Report 2026'. Image should appear at the top.
test_cases.expected_behavior: ImageRun has explicit type field. Image appears in document. File is valid.

test_cases.name: Footnotes — proper docx-js API
test_cases.prompt: Create a research summary Word document with 3 paragraphs. Add footnotes: paragraph 1 cites 'Smith et al. 2024', paragraph 2 cites 'See appendix A', paragraph 3 cites 'Data from WHO 2025'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document has footnotes section in XML (word/footnotes.xml). FootnoteReferenceRun used correctly. Footnotes appear at page bottom.

test_cases.name: US Letter page size explicit — not A4 default
test_cases.prompt: Create a US Letter format business letter (8.5" x 11") with 1-inch margins. Include a letterhead, date, recipient address, 2-paragraph body, and signature block.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Page size in XML is exactly 12240 width x 15840 height DXA. Margins are 1440 DXA (1 inch) on all sides.

test_cases.name: Numbered list — independent vs continuing references
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with two separate numbered lists. List 1: Steps to install (3 items). List 2: Steps to configure (4 items). Each list must start numbering from 1 independently.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Two distinct numbering references used so each list restarts at 1. No shared reference that would cause list 2 to start at 4.

test_cases.name: Extract all text with structure preserved
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and extract all its text content. Preserve the heading hierarchy in your output.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model uses pandoc for extraction. Output shows heading levels (# ## ###) matching the fixture. All paragraph text present.

test_cases.name: Extract table data as structured output
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and extract the table data as a JSON array of objects, using the header row as keys.
test_cases.expected_behavior: JSON output with correct keys from header row. All data rows present. Values match fixture table content.

test_cases.name: Summarize document — read then write
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and create a new 1-page executive summary Word document based on its content.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model reads source with pandoc first, then creates new .docx. Summary contains key points from fixture. Output is valid .docx.

test_cases.name: Read and compute statistics from table
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document, extract the numeric data from the table, and report: sum of each column, average of each column, and which row has the highest total.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Computed values match actual numbers in fixture table. No invented values.

test_cases.name: Find and replace — unpack→edit→pack workflow
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached Word document and replace all occurrences of 'Company' with 'Acme Corp'. Preserve all formatting.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model unpacks with unpack.py, uses str_replace on document.xml directly, repacks with pack.py --original. All 6 instances replaced. No formatting lost.

test_cases.name: Add tracked changes (w:ins / w:del)
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached contract Word document and use tracked changes to: (1) change '30 days' to '60 days' in the Payment Terms section, (2) delete the sentence containing 'Party A reserves the right'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Changes appear as tracked (w:ins/w:del in XML). Original text remains readable. Document is valid.

test_cases.name: Add comments to specific text
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached document and add a comment to the text 'Methodology' saying 'Clarify this section with recent research'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Comment appears in word/comments.xml. Document is valid. Comment attached to correct location.

test_cases.name: Change text formatting — bold, italic, underline
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached document. Find all instances of the word 'important' and make them bold. Find all instances of 'Note:' and make them italic and underline.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Formatting applied correctly. No other text modified. Document valid.

test_cases.name: Insert header and footer
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached document. Add a header with the text 'CONFIDENTIAL' centered, and a footer with page numbers centered.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Header and footer added. Page numbers auto-increment. Document valid.

test_cases.name: Merge/combine multiple documents
test_cases.prompt: You have two Word documents available: simple_report.docx and newsletter_raw.docx. Merge them into a single document with simple_report.docx content first, then newsletter_raw.docx content on a new page.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Both documents' content appears. Page break between them. Output is single valid .docx.

test_cases.name: Convert .doc to .docx
test_cases.prompt: Convert the attached legacy .doc file to .docx format. Preserve all formatting, tables, and content.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output file is valid .docx (OOXML ZIP structure). All content from original present. No corruption.

test_cases.name: Convert .docx to Markdown
test_cases.prompt: Convert the attached Word document to Markdown format. Preserve heading hierarchy, tables (as ASCII tables), and bold/italic formatting using Markdown syntax.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is valid Markdown. Headings use #, ##, ### syntax. Tables converted to ASCII/Markdown table format. All content present.

test_cases.name: Empty document — valid but minimal
test_cases.prompt: Create an empty Word document with just default formatting. File should be valid and openable.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output file is valid .docx. Has default page setup. No errors when opened in Word.

test_cases.name: Very large document — many pages
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with 50 pages of Lorem Ipsum text. Include page breaks between every 5 pages.
test_cases.expected_behavior: File is valid .docx. Page count is 50. File size reasonable (no bloat). Opens without errors.

test_cases.name: Special characters — Unicode and symbols
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document containing text with various special characters: emoji (😀🎉), math symbols (∑∫√), currency (€¥₹), and other Unicode: 你好 (Chinese), مرحبا (Arabic).
test_cases.expected_behavior: All characters render correctly. Document is valid. No corruption or mojibake.

test_cases.name: Mixed content — text, images, tables, all together
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with: title heading, intro paragraph, a 2x2 table with mixed content (text and numbers), an image below the table, conclusion text, and footer with page number.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All elements present and properly formatted. Layout is clean. Document is valid.

test_cases.name: Tracked deletions — read and preserve
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document that contains tracked deletions. Output a summary showing: (1) what text was deleted, (2) who made the changes, (3) whether the deletions are still visible.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Correctly identifies all deleted text. Author names extracted. Shows understanding of tracked changes state.
